What ¥1000
Buys You

spoiler: way more than you'd expect ✦

Conbini Christmas haul¥~300

POSSIBILITIESINFINITE
✦ Japan wonevery time
verified by experience
01

The Conbini Christmas Miracle

Seven-Eleven · right outside school · 5pm energy

¥~900 split btw friends

Starbucks Chilled Latte + Xmas Log Cake

The Lawson right in front of school during Christmas season is genuinely unfair. That Uchi Café log cake — the little snowman guy on top — was sitting there every afternoon calling my name. We bought one, grabbed some cookies, dragged chairs together in the classroom at 5pm when all the teachers had gone, and just... ate cake in an empty Japanese classroom like it was completely normal. It was one of the best evenings of the exchange.

this is peak japan budget dining. seasonal conbini items are NOT a drill 🎄
02

The Aesthetic Café Hangout

A danish + a latte + a friend + a window seat = perfection

¥~500

Danish + Latte at Yoridokoro

Japan has this category of café that just feels like someone designed it specifically to make you feel like the main character. Flaky pastry, perfectly foamed drink, wooden tray, huge window. Under ¥600 in a lot of places. The Danish was buttery and golden and everything. The latte had a latte art heart on it. It costs less than a mediocre coffee back home and it comes with a whole vibe included.

yoridokoro translated = "place to rely on" which is exactly right 🥐
03

Takoyaki from the Station Mall

fresh, saucy, burning-hot octopus balls. worth every yen.

¥~600

6-piece Takoyaki Set

You know that feeling of burning your tongue and not caring even a little bit? That's takoyaki from a station mall stall. Crispy outside, molten inside, draped in okonomiyaki sauce and Japanese mayo, topped with dancing bonito flakes that move in the steam like they're alive. The wooden boat tray is part of the experience. Standing there eating it while crowds of commuters rush past — deeply, specifically Japanese moment.

they ARE hot. you WILL burn your mouth. you WILL order again anyway 🐙

The Jump Shop
is a religious
experience.

Tokyo Station has a Jump Shop and if you go in there with ¥1000 you will leave with something. Guaranteed. A keychain, a clearfile, a tiny acrylic stand. I walked in for "a look" and left with a Demon Slayer pretzel snack and a new personality. Also my JJK tote bag that I wear everywhere — secondhand, mint condition, ¥300. That's it. That's the tweet.

04

The Secondhand Goldmine

Book-Off & vintage shops — where ¥1000 becomes legendary

The Full Haul
Collab Snacks
Sumikko Stamps
Mildliners
Matching Set
¥700

Kanna to Deco Set — Mint Condition

Full manga set. Complete. Every volume. Mint condition. At a secondhand Book-Off for ¥700. This is not a drill. Japan's secondhand book culture is so deeply, obsessively well-maintained — people sell manga they've read once and the next person gets it practically new. I found a whole series for the price of a coffee at home.

Book-Off is not optional. It is a pilgrimage. 📚
¥300

JJK Tote Bag — Secondhand, Mint

That canvas tote with the JJK panel art? The one I'm wearing in the Jump Shop photo? ¥300. Three hundred yen. That's barely over two USD. Found it in a secondhand anime goods shop alongside Chibi Debi manga, acrylic keychains, and all sorts of other treasures. Japan's secondhand market for merch is shockingly good and criminally underrated.

also got: chibi debi manga + acrylic charms 🎭
¥~600

Matching Penguin Keychains

The whole point of these was to get one each. My friend took the blue one, I took the grey one. Two little round penguins standing together in different hands at a toy shop — I genuinely got a bit emotional about it and I will not apologise. It's the most Japanese exchange student thing to do and I would do it again immediately.

friendship in keychain form 🐧🩵

How to max out your ¥1000

from someone who field-tested this extensively

01

Go to Book-Off

Japan's massive secondhand bookstore chain. Manga in mint condition for ¥100–¥300 each. Complete sets exist. Go in with a list or lose control completely.

02

Seasonal Conbini Is King

Christmas, Valentine's, hanami season — Japanese convenience stores release limited items that are genuinely special. Don't sleep on Seven-Eleven's Uchi Café line.

03

Secondhand Anime Shops

Not just Book-Off — dedicated secondhand merch shops near otaku districts have insane finds. Tote bags, acrylic stands, clearfiles for ¥100–¥500 each.

04

Train Station Food Courts

Takoyaki, taiyaki, crepes — station malls often have fresh made-to-order stuff at surprisingly reasonable prices. Walk slowly and look around.

05

Get The Matching Thing

Whatever it is — keychains, matching pins, a coordinating sticker — buy it. You'll have it for years. It's worth every yen and then some.

06

Collab Stationery

Shinchan Mildliners. Sanrio Zebra pens. Limited edition anything. Japanese stationery collabs are a whole genre and they last forever if you take care of them.

季節
Seasonal Buying Guide · 季節のおすすめ
WINTER · 冬
Christmas log cakes at Lawson. Seven-Eleven's Uchi Café snowman series. Limited strawberry milk drinks. Hot corn soup in vending machines.
SPRING · 春
Sakura-flavored everything (Starbucks Sakura Latte, sakura mochi). Strawberry season produces the best conbini strawberry items. Hanami snack packs.
SUMMER · 夏
Kakigori (shaved ice) stalls appear everywhere. Cold melon pan. Edamame snacks. Limitless cold matcha variations. Tanabata festival conbini drops.
AUTUMN · 秋
Sweet potato season — the best imo (芋) flavoured items appear. Mont Blanc cake at every conbini. Chestnut (kuri) everything. Pumpkin season.
